channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: A Cancer Detection Test Fails in Major Study
item.description: A closely watched clinical trial in Britain that screened blood for early detection of cancer did not show a reduction in diagnoses at later stages of...

item.title: Wall Street shrugs at widely expected Supreme Court tariff ruling
item.description: The S&P 500 was 0.1% higher in midday trading.

item.title: CFO whose business fell 10% from tariffs cheers Supreme Court ruling: ‘No single person should have unilateral authority over tariffs’
item.description: Matt Totsch, the CFO of manufacturer Trim-Tex, said businesses like his have been dealing with crippling uncertainty caused by Trump’s chaotic tariff ...

item.title: How Jeffrey Epstein exploited colleges’ ‘tremendous drive to acquire money’
item.description: Schools try to make sure there is no financial conflict of interest for the donor, researcher or institution. Emphasis on the "try."

item.title: Will the Supreme Court’s Tariff Ruling Curb Trump?
item.description: What does this mean for the president, the economy — and your bank account?

item.title: Meet ‘Your Rich BFF,’ the former JPMorgan trader and TikTok star who wants you to talk about money on the first date
item.description: Vivian Tu, who refers to herself as “your favorite Wall Street girly,” also hosts the “Networth and Chill" podcast and published a book, “Well Endowed...

item.title: Christopher S. Wren, Times Bureau Chief in Hostile Lands, Dies at 89
item.description: Over three decades, he reported from Moscow, Beijing, Tehran and elsewhere and wrote well-received books based on his reporting, including one about h...

item.title: The Supreme Court’s Declaration of Independence
item.description: The court’s rejection of President Trump’s tariffs program is the latest in a series of clashes between him and Chief Justice John G. Roberts Jr.

item.title: India’s Hindu Right Seems Unstoppable. This City Shows How.
item.description: Muslims make up a majority in Sambhal, but after deadly clashes over a mosque, they say they the arms of the state are now stifling them.

item.title: New York nurses union wins 12% raise, AI safeguards in a tentative deal to end monthlong strike
item.description: Over the past month, nurses had picketed during bitterly cold temperatures in what their union said was the largest and longest walkout in the city’s ...

item.title: Johannes Dale-Skjevdal of Norway Wins Biathlon Mass Start
item.description: Johannes Dale-Skjevdal of Norway hit all 20 of his shots to win the gold medal in the mass start event.

item.title: An Enemy’s Fall Frees Up South Korea’s Leader. Now Comes the Tough Part.
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung gained legitimacy from his predecessor’s conviction​. But South Korea’s political polarization is caustic, and could get worse...

item.title: India’s Hindu Right Seems Unstoppable. This City Shows How.
item.description: Muslims make up a majority in Sambhal, but after deadly clashes over a mosque, they say they the arms of the state are now stifling them.

item.title: Hungary Poses Unexpected Hurdle to Europe’s 90-Billion Euro Loan to Ukraine
item.description: While the delay may prove to be procedural, Hungary signaled that it could cause problems as the European Union works to send money to Ukraine.

item.title: U.K. Police Contacting Security Officers Who Once Protected Former Prince Andrew
item.description: The authorities also searched a mansion used by Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, who was arrested in connection with an investigation into suspicion of mis...
